mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 645Ci engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 645Ci (N62B44) engine typically costs £3,000–£5,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A blown engine can cost £9,000–£13,000 to replace. Valve stem seal replacement costs around £790–£3,500, while timing chain guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

The N62 V8 has a mixed reputation. It's 'a strong dependable engine' but 'burdened with complex engineering and weak points that lead to expensive repairs'. Compared to the inline-six 630i, ownership demands more frequent and costly maintenance. Well-documented weaknesses include valve stem seals, timing chain guides, oil leaks, and cooling system failures.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 645Ci?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Valve stem seal failure — causing blue smoke on start-up and heavy oil consumption, (2) Timing chain guide and tensioner failure — causing rattle and risk of catastrophic engine damage, (3) Coolant crossover pipe failure — requiring significant disassembly, (4) Alternator bracket seal leaks, and (5) Multiple oil leaks from valve covers, timing covers and oil filter housing.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 645Ci engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £3,000–£6,000 645Ci, a £4,000–£5,500 recon is 65–180% of the car's value. For a clean example with good body and no rust, replacement may be worthwhile given the E63's strong following. However, if the car has other issues (iDrive, transmission, electrical gremlins), evaluate total restoration costs before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 645Ci engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 645Ci uses the N62B44 engine family. Engine codes include N62B44A (2003–2004, 320 hp) and N62B44B (2004–2005, 333 hp). The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N62 valve stem seals, or does it need engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — valve stem seals can be replaced without removing the engine using specialist tools. The repair typically costs £790–£3,500. A company in Nuneaton offers this service for approximately £790 + VAT, including rocker cover gaskets and timing chain gaskets. If caught early, this repair can restore the engine to full health and prevent fouled spark plugs and damaged catalytic converters.

eraMap.title: BMW 645Ci — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 4.4L V8 — 320 hp, 440 Nm. Valve stem seals, timing chain guides and alternator bracket leaks are the main weak points
4.4L V8 — 333 hp, 450 Nm. Same N62 family with revised ECU calibration

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 645Ci engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The N62's complex architecture and tight engine bay make it a significant job.

problems.title: Valve Stem Seal Failure (The Most Notorious N62 Issue)
problems.symptoms: Blue smoke from exhaust on cold start, heavy oil consumption, fouled spark plugs, failed emissions test, visible smoke after deceleration
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–130,000 miles — seals become brittle with age and heat
problems.repairCost: £790–£3,500 (valve stem seal replacement using specialist tool kit)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if oil consumption becomes excessive

problems.recommendation: Valve stem seal failure is the most notorious issue on the N62 engine. Originally equipped seals become brittle over time, no longer sealing to the valve stem, leading to heavy oil consumption and blue smoke on start-up. This is a labour-intensive job requiring specialist tools to replace the seals. A company in Nuneaton offers this service for approximately £790 + VAT. If you notice blue smoke or excessive oil consumption, have the seals inspected. Catching this early prevents fouled spark plugs, damaged catalytic converters, and more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full valve stem seal guide

problems.title: Timing Chain Guide & Tensioner Failure
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the front of the engine on cold start, chain "slap" sound, metallic noise, plastic particles in oil sump
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — guides become brittle and crack
problems.replacementCost: £5,500–£8,500 (if chain jumps or snaps — full engine replacement)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The timing chain guides and tensioners on the N62 4.4L engine are a common failure point. One owner reported: "The timing chain tensioners failed (another known bmw alert) £6000 later". The plastic guides become brittle with age and heat, cracking and allowing the chain to become slack. If left unattended, the timing chain can jump, causing pistons to hit valves and resulting in catastrophic engine damage. At the first sign of rattle, get the chain inspected immediately. A £1,500–£3,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£8,500 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Multiple Points)
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell, oil spots under the car, visible oil residue around the engine, low oil level warning
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,500 (gasket replacement — valve cover, timing cover, alternator bracket)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice oil spots or burning smell

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are one of the most common complaints on the N62. The valve cover gaskets, upper timing cover gaskets, alternator bracket gasket, and oil filter housing gaskets are all known to leak. One owner reported: "within 1,500 miles a smell of burning oil was coming into the cabin... problem was rocker cover gaskets leaking". The alternator bracket seal leak is particularly frustrating — repairs are time-intensive due to component placement. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the gaskets inspected promptly. Catching leaks early prevents oil contamination of other components and more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide

problems.title: Coolant Crossover Pipe & Cooling System Failures
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating, steam from engine bay, temperature gauge rising
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if temperature rises

problems.recommendation: The coolant crossover pipe, located deep within the engine valley, is a known failure point on the N62. It cracks due to constant heat cycling, and repair often involves significant disassembly and high labour costs. The electric water pump is also prone to failure with little or no warning. If the temperature gauge rises, shut the engine down immediately — overheating an all-aluminium N62 can cause head gasket failure or worse. Preventative replacement of the water pump and thermostat at 60,000–80,000 miles is strongly recommended.

failureLink.label: Read the full cooling system guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 645Ci? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with BMW-approved oil changes (5W-30, 8 litres)
* Evidence of recent valve stem seal replacement — this is the most notorious N62 issue
* Timing chain guides and tensioners already replaced — this is critical
avoidIf: * Blue smoke on start-up — indicates valve stem seal failure
* Rattling noise from the front of the engine — timing chain guide failure
* Overheating history or coolant system issues — the N62 is unforgiving of overheating
* No service history — V8 BMWs are expensive to maintain; lack of history is a red flag

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 645Ci's N62 V8 is a charismatic engine — a 4.4-litre naturally aspirated V8 with a glorious soundtrack and up to 333 hp. Compared to the inline-six 630i, the V8 is "burdened with complex engineering and weak points that lead to expensive repairs" and "ownership demands more frequent and costly maintenance". Valve stem seal failure is the most notorious issue, followed by timing chain guide failure, oil leaks, and cooling system problems. As one owner warned: "If you insist on getting a 6 series don't get the V8 petrol variant!" Buy a well-maintained example with documented preventative work — especially valve stem seals and timing chain guides — or budget for these repairs immediately. Without history? The repair bills can be eye-watering.

cta.label: → Compare 645Ci engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 645Ci (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (timing chain guides) and blue smoke (valve stem seals)
* Oil Consumption — Ask about oil usage; 1 litre per 1,000 miles suggests valve stem seal wear
* Service History — Including the N62B44 engine and confirmed BMW-approved oil changes
* Timing Chain History — Has the chain, guides and tensioners been replaced? This is critical
* Valve Stem Seals — Have they been replaced? This is the most notorious N62 issue
* Cooling System — Check for coolant leaks; ask about water pump and thermostat history
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the valve covers, timing covers and alternator bracket
* Electrical Gremlins — Check iDrive, ABS, traction control — E63s are known for electrical issues

items.question: Is the BMW 645Ci engine reliable?
items.answer: The N62 V8 has a mixed reputation. It's "a strong dependable engine" but "burdened with complex engineering and weak points that lead to expensive repairs". Compared to the inline-six 630i, ownership demands more frequent and costly maintenance. Well-documented weaknesses include valve stem seals, timing chain guides, oil leaks, and cooling system failures. With preventative maintenance, the engine can last ~320,000 km (200,000 miles).

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW 645Ci?
items.answer: The main known issues are: (1) Valve stem seal failure — causing blue smoke on start-up and heavy oil consumption, (2) Timing chain guide and tensioner failure — causing rattle and risk of catastrophic engine damage, (3) Coolant crossover pipe failure — requiring significant disassembly, (4) Alternator bracket seal leaks, and (5) Multiple oil leaks from valve covers, timing covers and oil filter housing.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW 645Ci engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £3,000–£6,000 645Ci, a £4,000–£5,500 recon is 65–180% of the car's value. For a clean example with good body and no rust, replacement may be worthwhile given the E63's strong following. However, if the car has other issues (iDrive, transmission, electrical gremlins), evaluate total restoration costs before committing. For examples under £3,000, the replacement cost may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

items.answer: Yes — valve stem seals can be replaced without removing the engine using specialist tools. The repair typically costs £790–£3,500. A company in Nuneaton offers this service for approximately £790 + VAT, including rocker cover gaskets and timing chain gaskets.
